cases.case_ref: Prysmian Group vs. Sterlite Technologies Inc. -- trade secret misappropriation
cases.forum: U.S. District Court, South Carolina (jury trial); District Court affirmance 29 Aug 2025; appeal process ongoing
cases.parties: Prysmian Group (plaintiff) vs. Sterlite Technologies Inc. (US subsidiary) and Stephen Szymanski (former Prysmian executive who joined Sterlite in 2020)
cases.note: Prysmian filed suit June 2021 alleging Szymanski took confidential customer, product and manufacturing-expansion information when joining Sterlite. A South Carolina jury awarded Prysmian USD96.5mn (~INR800cr+) against Sterlite Technologies Inc. and USD200,000 against Szymanski personally, 9 Aug 2024. The District Court affirmed the verdict on 29 Aug 2025 (denying Prysmian's request for prejudgment interest). Sterlite has publicly stated it intends to pursue further appeal; the sources reviewed for this report do not show a final appellate resolution as of July 2026 -- this should be treated as 'disposed unfavorable at trial/district-court level, with an active appeal in progress' rather than fully closed. Independently flagged as an ongoing risk in CRISIL's 16 Jun 2026 rating rationale.
cases.source: Jury verdict 9 Aug 2024; District Court order affirming verdict (and rejecting Prysmian's prejudgment-interest claim) 29 Aug 2025; Sterlite has stated intent to appeal

cases.case_ref: Sterlite Technologies vs. Fujikura -- European patent dispute (EP 3796060)
cases.forum: European Patent Office, Technical Board of Appeal, Germany
cases.note: The EPO Technical Board of Appeal revoked Fujikura's European Patent EP 3796060 in its entirety, ruling in STL's favor and resolving related UK patent litigation over STL's Celesta cable family. STL states it is now pursuing recovery of its legal defence costs. This is the inverse of the Prysmian matter above -- a favorable outcome for STL, not a risk.
cases.source: EPO Technical Board of Appeal ruling, 24 Jun 2026 (final, binding, non-appealable)
